content: Create an article outline for 'Modular Capability Providers vs. Skills: How AI Agents Get Their Superpowers', focusing on: 1) Definitions and contrasts of MCPs vs. skills, 2) how they support each other, 3) differences between Claude code and Codex skill requirements and patterns, 4) implementation best practices and security, 5) practical examples, 6) guidance for agent builders.

### X. Modular Capability Providers vs. Skills: How AI Agents Get Their Superpowers

**Synopsis**
This article demystifies the core building blocks of contemporary AI agents—Modular Capability Providers (MCPs) and skills—clarifying their definitions, differences, and how they interact. It offers a practical guide for agent builders, contrasting the requirements and patterns of Claude Code and Codex agents, with actionable insights on implementation best practices and security considerations. Through real-world examples and design guidance, readers will learn how to architect secure, extensible, and powerful AI agents.

**Lead Intro**
When AI agents solve real business problems—automating workflows, reviewing code, or managing cloud deployments—it’s not just the model’s intelligence at work. Behind the scenes, a careful choreography unfolds: instructions coded as reusable “skills” are seamlessly coupled with Modular Capability Providers, granting access to email servers, cloud APIs, or secure data. But as agents become more ambitious, the line between what an agent knows (skills) and what it can do in the world (MCPs) matters more than ever. Misunderstandings here can mean brittle bots, security nightmares, or missed opportunities for innovation. Stepping behind the curtain, we see why the future of robust AI agents lies in getting this separation—and integration—exactly right.

**Target Audience**
Engineering Lead

**Key Takeaway**
By rigorously separating instructional skills from Modular Capability Providers—and following cross-platform best practices—agent developers can achieve secure, composable, and future-proof automation.

**Salable Hook**
Demonstrates expertise in building secure, extensible AI agent architectures—empowering teams to ship robust, compliant automations faster.

**Supporting Material**
- [Salable product documentation: Integrating secure agent capabilities](https://beta.salable.app/docs/agents/secure-capabilities)
- [Anthropic Claude Code Documentation (skills and MCP architecture)](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/skills-reference)

- [Internal: Best Practices for Agent Skills and Secure MCP Integration](https://beta.salable.app/docs/agent-skills-best-practices)

**Estimated Word Count**: 2,000 words

**Content Pillar**: Billing Operations
